task_completion.description: The document is saved with the correct filename.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The title bar of the word processor shows 'Light_in_Motion_Catalog.odt' or the file is visible in the file manager under /home/ga/Documents.

task_completion.description: An auto-generated Table of Contents is present at the beginning of the document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A structured Table of Contents is visible at the start of the document, typically with dotted leader lines connecting section titles to page numbers. In LibreOffice, it may have a gray background indicating it is an auto-generated field.

task_completion.description: Main sections are formatted with Heading 1 style.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Sections such as 'Curator's Foreword', 'Exhibition Overview', 'Loan Acknowledgments', and 'Inventory Checklist' are present and formatted with a large, prominent font size indicative of a Heading 1 style. The style dropdown in the toolbar may show 'Heading 1' when the cursor is on these titles.

task_completion.description: Artwork titles are formatted with Heading 2 style.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 8 artwork titles (e.g., 'Impression, Sunrise', 'The Dance Class') are present in the catalog section and formatted with a font size smaller than Heading 1 but larger than normal text, indicative of a Heading 2 style. The style dropdown may show 'Heading 2'.

task_completion.description: An Inventory Checklist is included as a table.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Under the 'Inventory Checklist' section, there is a visible table (a grid with rows and columns) containing the list of artworks and their details.

task_completion.description: Page numbers are inserted in the footer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Numbers are visible at the bottom margin (footer area) of the document pages, separated from the main body text.

integrity.description: The agent used a word processor GUI to create and format the document.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the LibreOffice Writer interface being actively used, with menus, toolbars, and dialog boxes open, rather than terminal scripts generating the file.

integrity.description: The document contains the correct exhibition data from the JSON file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text includes specific details like the curator 'Dr. Elaine Whitford' and correct artwork titles, showing the agent actually read and used the provided JSON data rather than using placeholder text.

integrity.description: The Table of Contents and page numbers are genuinely generated features.
integrity.visual_evidence: The TOC has standard auto-generated formatting (like gray shading or precise alignment with leader dots), and page numbers are in the actual footer area, not just manually typed at the bottom of the main text body.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The exhibition is titled 'Light in Motion: Masterworks of French Impressionism'. The curator is Dr. Elaine Whitford. The 8 artworks include Monet's 'Impression, Sunrise', Renoir's 'Bal du moulin de la Galette', Degas' 'The Dance Class', Morisot's 'The Cradle', Caillebotte's 'Paris Street; Rainy Day', Cassatt's 'The Child's Bath', Pissarro's 'Boulevard Montmartre at Night', and Sisley's 'Bridge at Villeneuve-la-Garenne'. The document must be saved as /home/ga/Documents/Light_in_Motion_Catalog.odt.
